Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: parser_OpenAIAuditLogsAliasFunction Data Parser with template version 3.0.0
parser_OpenAIChatCompletionsAliasFunction Data Parser with template version 3.0.0

connectorUiConfig.title: OpenAI (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The OpenAI data connector enables you to ingest audit logs, chat completion data, or both from your OpenAI organization into Microsoft Sentinel through the OpenAI API. Each data type uses a separate REST API poller and requires a different API key type: **audit logs** (user actions, API key management, organization changes, security events) require an **organization-level admin API key**, while **chat completions** (model usage, token consumption, performance metrics) require a **project-level API key**. You may configure one or both data types independently. Data is collected into separate tables (OpenAIAuditLogs and OpenAIChatCompletions) for security monitoring, compliance analysis, and usage monitoring. Refer to [OpenAI API documentation](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference) for more information.

dataTypes.name: OpenAIAuditLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
OpenAIAuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: OpenAIChatCompletions
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
OpenAIChatCompletions
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

value: union OpenAIAuditLogs, OpenAIChatCompletions | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h) | take 1

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: OpenAI API access
customs.description: Each data type requires a different API key type. An **organization-level admin API key** is required for audit logs - these can be created in your OpenAI organization settings. A **project-level API key** is required for chat completions - these can be created under a specific project in the OpenAI dashboard. You may configure audit logs, chat completions, or both independently.

instructionSteps.title: Connection Information
instructionSteps.description: Details on the connections used to collect data from OpenAI's API.

parameters.content:
- **Audit Logs** (`OpenAIAuditLogs`):
    - Use **organization-level admin API keys**.
    - Audit logging must be enabled in your OpenAI organization settings. Organization owners can go to OpenAI's `Organization settings` -> `Data controls` -> `Data retention` to enable audit logging.
    - Once OpenAI audit logging is enabled, it cannot be disabled without contacting OpenAI support.
- **Chat Completions** (`OpenAIChatCompletions`):
    - Use **project-level API keys**.
    - Only chat completions created with the `store` parameter set to `true` will be collected.
    - Deleting stored chat completions while this connector is active may require you to disconnect and reconnect to reset the data collection state.

instructionSteps.title: Add OpenAI Audit Logs Connection
instructionSteps.description: Enter your OpenAI API credentials to collect audit logs data from OpenAI API.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Add OpenAI Audit Logs Connection
parameters.subtitle: Connect to OpenAI Audit Logs API

parameters.content:
## OpenAI Audit Logs Connection

Configure your OpenAI organization-level admin API key to collect audit logs (user actions, API key management, organization changes, security events).

> **Note:** Audit logs require an **organization-level admin API key**. These can be created in your OpenAI organization settings.

parameters.label: Audit Logs Connection Name
parameters.placeholder: e.g. My OpenAI Org - Audit Logs
parameters.name: auditLogsFriendlyName

parameters.label: Organization API Key
parameters.placeholder: Enter your OpenAI organization-level admin API key
parameters.name: orgApiKey

instructionSteps.title: Add OpenAI Chat Completions Connection
instructionSteps.description: Enter your OpenAI API credentials to collect chat completions data from OpenAI API.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Add OpenAI Chat Completions Connection
parameters.subtitle: Connect to OpenAI Chat Completions API

parameters.content:
## OpenAI Chat Completions Connection

Configure your OpenAI project-level API key to collect chat completions data (model usage, token consumption, performance metrics).

> **Note:** Chat completions require a **project-level API key**. These can be created under a specific project in the OpenAI dashboard. Only chat completions created with the `store` parameter set to `true` will be collected.

parameters.label: Chat Completions Connection Name
parameters.placeholder: e.g. My OpenAI Project - Chat Completions
parameters.name: chatCompletionsFriendlyName

parameters.label: Project API Key
parameters.placeholder: Enter your OpenAI project-level API key
parameters.name: projApiKey

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(['effective_at']), now(), datetime(1970-01-01) + (['effective_at'] * 1s)) , Id = ['id'] , EventType = ['type'] , EffectiveAt = datetime(1970-01-01) + (['effective_at'] * 1s) , ActorType = tostring(actor.type) , ActorApiKey = todynamic(actor.api_key) , ActorSession = todynamic(actor.session) , Project = ['project'] , ConnectorName = ['friendlyName'] | project TimeGenerated , Id , EventType , EffectiveAt , ActorType , ActorApiKey , ActorSession , Project , EventDetails , ConnectorName

source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(['created']), now(), datetime(1970-01-01) + (['created'] * 1s)) , Id = ['id'] , ObjectType = ['object'] , CreatedAt = datetime(1970-01-01) + (['created'] * 1s) , Model = ['model'] , RequestId = ['request_id'] , ServiceTier = ['service_tier'] , SystemFingerprint = ['system_fingerprint'] , Choices = ['choices'] , Usage = ['usage'] , Metadata = ['metadata'] , ToolChoice = ['tool_choice'] , Tools = ['tools'] , Temperature = ['temperature'] , TopProbability = ['top_p'] , FrequencyPenalty = ['frequency_penalty'] , PresencePenalty = ['presence_penalty'] , Seed = ['seed'] , InputUser = ['input_user'] , ResponseFormat = ['response_format'] , ConnectorName = ['friendlyName'] | project TimeGenerated , Id , ObjectType , CreatedAt , Model , RequestId , ServiceTier , SystemFingerprint , Choices , Usage , Metadata , ToolChoice , Tools , Temperature , TopProbability , FrequencyPenalty , PresencePenalty , Seed , InputUser , ResponseFormat , ConnectorName

schema.name: OpenAIChatCompletions_CL
schema.description: OpenAI Chat Completions data collected from OpenAI Chat Completions API

columns.name: Id
columns.description: The unique identifier for the chat completion

columns.name: ObjectType
columns.description: The object type

columns.name: CreatedAt
columns.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created

columns.name: Model
columns.description: The model used for the chat completion

columns.name: RequestId
columns.description: The unique request identifier

columns.name: ServiceTier
columns.description: The processing type used for serving the request

columns.name: SystemFingerprint
columns.description: Backend configuration fingerprint

columns.name: Choices
columns.description: The list of chat completion choices

columns.name: Usage
columns.description: Usage statistics for the completion request

columns.name: Metadata
columns.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object

columns.name: ToolChoice
columns.description: Which tool (if any) was called by the model

columns.name: Tools
columns.description: List of tools the model may call

columns.name: Temperature
columns.description: The sampling temperature used

columns.name: TopProbability
columns.description: The nucleus sampling parameter

columns.name: FrequencyPenalty
columns.description: Frequency penalty parameter

columns.name: PresencePenalty
columns.description: Presence penalty parameter

columns.name: Seed
columns.description: The seed used for deterministic sampling

columns.name: InputUser
columns.description: User identifier for the request

columns.name: ResponseFormat
columns.description: The format specification for the response

columns.name: ConnectorName
columns.description: The friendly name of the connector instance that collected this log

schema.name: OpenAIAuditLogs_CL
schema.description: OpenAI Chat Completions data collected from OpenAI Chat Completions API

columns.name: Id
columns.description: The ID of this log

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: The event type

columns.name: EffectiveAt
columns.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of the event

columns.name: ActorType
columns.description: The actor who performed the audit logged action

columns.name: ActorApiKey
columns.description: The API Key used to perform the audit logged action

columns.name: ActorSession
columns.description: The session in which the audit logged action was performed

columns.name: Project
columns.description: The project that the action was scoped to

columns.name: EventDetails
columns.description: Details of the audit log event

columns.name: ConnectorName
columns.description: The friendly name of the connector instance that collected this log

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The OpenAI solution for Microsoft Sentinel enables you to ingest OpenAI events into Microsoft Sentinel.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

• Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Framework

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 2

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
